# OpenPet GitHub Repository Profile

Use this file when updating GitHub About, release pages, repository topics, and short recommendation copy. Keep the wording aligned with current release evidence: OpenPet is macOS-first for packaged validation, and Windows is not release-ready until signed Windows smoke evidence exists.

## GitHub About Description

Programmable Electron desktop pet platform with Control Center, AI chat, Creator Studio, plugins, pet packs, and local agent APIs.

## Website

https://github.com/dengyie/OpenPet#readme

## Topics

```text
electron
desktop-pet

plugins

creator-studio
local-first
mcp
openai-compatible
desktop-app
```

## Short Recommendation

OpenPet is a desktop pet project built as a real local platform: transparent pet window, React Control Center, AI provider settings, plugin lifecycle controls, pet-pack runtime, Creator Studio workflows, and repeatable release-evidence tooling.

## Longer Recommendation

OpenPet is for users and developers who want a desktop companion that can grow beyond a single renderer demo. It combines an Electron main-process service layer with a React Control Center, OpenAI-compatible AI settings, switchable pet packs, Creator Studio generation/review flows, local plugin execution controls, loopback-only automation APIs, and a documented release-evidence path.

The project is intentionally honest about its platform boundaries: API keys stay in the main process, local plugins are explicit and logged, third-party code is not described as fully sandboxed, and Windows release readiness is blocked until signed artifact smoke evidence is archived.

## Social / Release Blurb

OpenPet turns a desktop pet into a programmable local platform. It ships with a visual Control Center, AI chat, pet packs, Creator Studio, plugin runtime controls, local agent APIs, and a growing regression/evidence suite for production-minded Electron development.

## Claim Boundaries

- macOS is the current primary validated packaged-app path.
- Windows build and smoke-evidence tooling exists, but Windows is not release-ready without signed artifact evidence and real smoke reports.
- Provider presets are endpoint templates unless backed by archived provider smoke evidence.
- Local plugins are permission-gated and lifecycle-managed, but arbitrary local process execution is not a complete sandbox guarantee.
